methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $350M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

data.narrative:
GrayMatter Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ariyan Kabir and Brual Shah, currently valued at $300M+ post-money on $70M+ total capital raised. The peer universe comprises 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable, with five key private peers spanning Dexterity, Bedrock Robotics, 1X Technologies, Collaborative Robotics, and Fairmat.

Two valuation methods triangulate the fair value range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $300M last post-money, yielding $570M. Peer-median methodology examines five same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.6B, though the peer set exhibits significant dispersion (range: $350M to $10.0B), reflecting heterogeneous company maturity and capital intensity within frontier robotics.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $484M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.1B. The wide peer range and stage-progression methodology's lower output suggest material uncertainty around fair value, particularly given the sector's nascent market dynamics and heterogeneous business models. Key uncertainties include the target's positioning relative to outliers like 1X Technologies ($10.0B) versus lower-quartile peers like Fairmat ($350M), and the applicability of traditional stage-progression multiples in a capital-intensive, pre-commercialization sector.
